recipient"},"Redeem(address,uint256,address,address,address,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Event emitted when an asset is redeemed"},"RestrictedStatusChanged(address,bool)":{"notice":"Emitted when a restricted address status changes"},"SignerStatusChanged(address,bool)":{"notice":"Emitted when allowed signer status changes"}},"kind":"user","methods":{"ADMIN_ROLE()":{"notice":"Admin role can add new collateral types"},"GATEKEEPER_ROLE()":{"notice":"Gatekeeper role can pause and unpause functionality"},"MAGICVALUE()":{"notice":"MAGICVALUE to be used in ERC1271 verification"},"MINTER_ROLE()":{"notice":"Minter role can call mint() and redeem() functions"},"VERSION()":{"notice":"Semantic version"},"asset()":{"notice":"Asset token this controller manages"},"custodian()":{"notice":"Address to transfer the custody portion of collateral to"},"delegates(address,address)":{"notice":"Accounts which have delegated a signer to sign orders on their behalf"},"hashOrder((uint8,uint256,uint256,address,address,address,uint256,uint256))":{"notice":"Get the EIP712 typed data hash of an order."},"isCollateral(address)":{"notice":"Supported collateral tokens"},"isRestricted(address)":{"notice":"Mapping of restricted accounts"},"manager()":{"notice":"Address to transfer the on-chain portion of collateral to"},"mint((uint8,uint256,uint256,address,address,address,uint256,uint256),(uint8,bytes))":{"notice":"Mint asset tokens"},"oracle()":{"notice":"The price oracle is used to prevent orders from exceeding a delta tolerance The oracle struct contains an oracle adapter to normalize price, and an oracle tolerance This is a security measure to prevent minting excessive tokens / redeeming at a price away from spot The oracle is not used to determine exact price, rather it enforces a price delta tolerance"},"pauseStatus()":{"notice":"Pause status"},"ratio()":{"notice":"Percentage of collateral to transfer to custodian"},"recipients(address,address)":{"notice":"Approved recipients are accounts set by a whitelisted signer to receive tokens"},"redeem((uint8,uint256,uint256,address,address,address,uint256,uint256),(uint8,bytes))":{"notice":"Redeem asset tokens"},"rescueToken(address,address)":{"notice":"Rescue tokens sent to this contract"},"setDelegateStatus(address,bool)":{"notice":"Allow an account to delegate a signer to sign orders on their behalf"},"setIsRestricted(address,bool)":{"notice":"Sets or unsets an address as restricted"},"setRecipientStatus(address,bool)":{"notice":"Set whether or not an account is a recipient for a given signer Recipients for a signer can receive tokens when an order is executed"},"setSignerStatus(address,bool)":{"notice":"Signer manager can set allowed signers"},"signers(address)":{"notice":"Whitelist for signer accounts"},"verifyNonce(address,uint256)":{"notice":"Verify a signer nonce."},"verifyOrder((uint8,uint256,uint256,address,address,address,uint256,uint256),(uint8,bytes))":{"notice":"Verify an order signed with EIP712"},"version()":{"notice":"Contract semantic version"}},"notice":"The Controller handles mint and redemption orders for the Tenbin protocol Tenbin is an asset token issuance platform with the goal of creating liquid, composable financial assets. Assets in the Tenbin protocol are backed by two positions: off-chain futures contracts and on-chain collateral. An off-chain hedging system maintains a delta one exposure of an underlying asset. On-chain collateral is used to earn low-risk yield. So long as the on-chain yield equals or exceeds the off-chain funding costs, the protocol is able to peg Tenbin assets to the spot price of the real asset. The controller contract is responsible for minting and redeeming assets in the Tenbin protocol Mint and redemptions are encoded as an Order. Orders are signed by KYC-approved signers and specify order details such as collateral amount, asset amount, and deadline. To successfully execute an order, a minter account calls the mint or redeem with an order and signature. Orders are executed atomically: collateral is transferred and tokens are minted/burned in a single transaction. All orders are executed by a minter key stored in a hardware security module and controlled by the Tenbin backend. When a mint is executed, the collateral is split between a custodian account and a manager account. The controller ratio represents the percentage of collateral to transfer to the the custodian account. The controller has several administrative functions to manage order signers, add order beneficiaries, and allow delegating to a signer. Signers are whitelisted by the SIGNER_MANAGER_ROLE. Once a signer is whitelisted, orders signed by this signer are valid so long as the signer == order.payer, or the payer has delegated to a signer. A signer can maintain a list of approved recipients. Only approved recipients for a signer can receive tokens during an order execution. The controller never holds any tokens - collateral is held in a CollateralManager contract or off-ramped by a custodian account. An oracle is used as a backstop to prevent order price from deviating from the oracle price. However, order price is not determined by the oracle on-chain. The controller is intended to be the only account which can mint asset tokens. In the case a new controller is created, the old controller is deprecated and minting permission is set to the new params.owner_: Account to set as the DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE
params.ratio_: Ratio of collateral transferred to custodian during mints

eip712Domain().details: returns the fields and values that describe the domain separator used by this contract for EIP-712 signature.

encodeOrder((uint8,uint256,uint256,addre.details: Encode order data according to EIP712 specification

getDomainSeparator().details: Get the domain separator for this contract

getRoleAdmin(bytes32).details: Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}.

grantRole(bytes32,address).details: Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event.

multicall(bytes[]).details: Allow batched calls to this contract

params.data: Data to delegatecall on this contract

renounceRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

rescueToken(address,address).details: The receiver should be a trusted address to avoid external calls attack vectors

params.token: The address of the ERC20 token to be rescued

revokeRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

setOracleAdapter(address).details: Change the oracle tolerance. 1e18 = 100% Setting the adapter to address(0) will disable it and reset the tolerance to zero

setOracleTolerance(uint96).details: Change the oracle price delta tolerance. 1e18 = 100%

setPauseStatus(uint8).details: Gatekeeper role can set pause status

setRatio(uint256).details: Change the ratio. The ratio must be 1-1e18.

params.recipient: Account to change recipient status for
params.status: True if an account is a valid recipient address for a signer

supportsInterface(bytes4).details: Returns true if this contract implements the interface defined by `interfaceId`. See the corresponding https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-165#how-interfaces-are-identified[ERC section] to learn more about how these ids are created. This function call must use less than 30 000 gas.

ADMIN_ROLE().notice: Admin role can add new collateral types

GATEKEEPER_ROLE().notice: Gatekeeper role can pause and unpause functionality

MAGICVALUE().notice: MAGICVALUE to be used in ERC1271 verification

MINTER_ROLE().notice: Minter role can call mint() and redeem() functions

custodian().notice: Address to transfer the custody portion of collateral to

delegates(address,address).notice: Accounts which have delegated a signer to sign orders on their behalf

hashOrder((uint8,uint256,uint256,address.notice: Get the EIP712 typed data hash of an order.

manager().notice: Address to transfer the on-chain portion of collateral to

oracle().notice: The price oracle is used to prevent orders from exceeding a delta tolerance The oracle struct contains an oracle adapter to normalize price, and an oracle tolerance This is a security measure to prevent minting excessive tokens / redeeming at a price away from spot The oracle is not used to determine exact price, rather it enforces a price delta tolerance

ratio().notice: Percentage of collateral to transfer to custodian

recipients(address,address).notice: Approved recipients are accounts set by a whitelisted signer to receive tokens

rescueToken(address,address).notice: Rescue tokens sent to this contract

setDelegateStatus(address,bool).notice: Allow an account to delegate a signer to sign orders on their behalf

setIsRestricted(address,bool).notice: Sets or unsets an address as restricted

setRecipientStatus(address,bool).notice: Set whether or not an account is a recipient for a given signer Recipients for a signer can receive tokens when an order is executed

setSignerStatus(address,bool).notice: Signer manager can set allowed signers

verifyOrder((uint8,uint256,uint256,addre.notice: Verify an order signed with EIP712
